response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced engineer with genuine developer-tooling credentials (worked on the LabWindows/CVI IDE at National Instruments, built test runners and libraries, low-latency systems at Dunnhumby), but the role's core requirements are largely unmet: his career centers on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, with only brief, dated Java exposure, no Kotlin, no Spring or enterprise JVM framework experience, and no demonstrated JVM depth. The IDE experience is over a decade old and in C/C++, not JVM tooling.
strengths: Direct IDE development experience (NI LabWindows/CVI) and clear interest in developer tooling
Strong open-source track record building language tooling and test frameworks (Trial, Fluent-Asserts)
Proven ability to work on complex, high-scale systems (millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby)
concerns: No Kotlin and no Spring/enterprise Java framework experience, both hard requirements
Java exposure is minimal, secondary, and roughly a decade old; no evidence of deep JVM expertise
Recent work (TypeScript/Node.js product engineering) is far from JVM/IDE tooling domain
